subject: Update blueprints to use k8splugin in version range >=3.4.3,<4.0.0
commit_message:

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

Though this change helps to limit bootstrap/blueprint revision during plugin development window, need to evaluate if any side-effect from release/testing standpoint (as plugin version is not strictly bound to plugin version loaded under CM).

Jack - Any thoughts? Recall there was some issue/conflict noted when plugin import was specified in range in prev rls.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Though this change helps to limit bootstrap/blueprint revision during plugin development window, need to evaluate if any side-effect from release/testing standpoint (as plugin version is not strictly bound to plugin version loaded under CM).
> 
> Jack - Any thoughts? Recall there was some issue/conflict noted when plugin import was specified in range in prev rls.

I don't remember the issue.  I have been using this range syntax for the simple blueprints I use for plugin testing.
When we make a release, it might be wise to use a fixed version for the plugin, just to make sure that every ONAP installation uses the same release.   (cm-container actually loads the plugins, so cm-container controls what versions are available.  If it installs only one version of the k8splugin, then there's no question about what version will be used, and then the range in the blueprint doesn't matter.)

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

If it works this seems to be a good solution for frequently changing plugin versions
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

Okay, lets move forward using the range spec as this will help during dev window. We can switch back to specific version if any issue is noticed.

Btw, there was new k8splugin 1.5.0 version merged recently  - pls check if import should be updated. 
Also include bootstrap version revision with this change.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Okay, lets move forward using the range spec as this will help during dev window. We can switch back to specific version if any issue is noticed.
> 
> Btw, there was new k8splugin 1.5.0 version merged recently  - pls check if import should be updated. 
> Also include bootstrap version revision with this change.

K8splugin is not integrated in cm-container (I think the team wanted to do that after completing all of IPv6 updates). Currently set version of bootstrap wasn't released yet.

Btw, one more advantage of keeping the version as a range (at least until the release sign-off) is that there won't be any interference if for example there are issues in gating due to update of cloudify plugin (now it requires coordinating bootstrap plugins update and cm-container update).

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> >

> > Btw, there was new k8splugin 1.5.0 version merged recently  - pls check if import should be updated. 
> > Also include bootstrap version revision with this change.
> 
> K8splugin is not integrated in cm-container (I think the team wanted to do that after completing all of IPv6 updates). Currently set version of bootstrap wasn't released yet.
> 
> Btw, one more advantage of keeping the version as a range (at least until the release sign-off) is that there won't be any interference if for example there are issues in gating due to update of cloudify plugin (now it requires coordinating bootstrap plugins update and cm-container update).

I'm okay with 1.5.0 k8splugin integration done later; we can proceed to merge once the version revision is done.
